chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA Present: Irshad Ali Shah, J. Agha Faisal, J. CP D 121 of 2010: Abdul Shakoor &Othersvs. E.D.O (CDD) &Others For the Petitioners: Mr. Fida Hussain Shah,Advocate For the Respondents: Mr.Abdul Hamid Bhurgri Additional Advocate General Dates of hearing: 02.03.2022 Date of announcement: 02.03.2022 ORDER Agha Faisal, J. The petitioners claim to have been rendering services to the respondents since the year 2010, however, without any salary or remuneration, hence, this petition. The learned Addl. A.G. drew attention to the comments of the respondents No.2 and 3 to demonstrate that the claim of employment by the petitioners was rested on fake and fabricated documents and that the petitioners have never performed any regular duty with the respondents. It is further stated that the posts to which the petitioners claim entitlement stood abolished. The learned Addl. A.G. has submitted that any employment in government service has to be predicated upon an advertised, competitive recruitment process open to all eligible candidates and that surreptitious employment otherwise than in accordance with a transparent recruitment process cannot be sanctioned. It is observed that the very employment of the petitioners has been denied by the respondents, therefore, the question of release of any salary would at best the premature in the present circumstances. Even otherwise, while the petitioners may seek to dispute the factum/ record pleaded by the respondents, the writ jurisdiction of this Court is not an amenable forum for such an exercise. In view hereof, we are of the considered view that no case has been set forth for the exercise of discretionary writ jurisdiction of this Court, hence, this petition is dismissed.

Even otherwise, while the petitioners may seek to dispute the factum/ record pleaded by the respondents, the writ jurisdiction of this Court is not an amenable forum for such an exercise. In view hereof, we are of the considered view that no case has been set forth for the exercise of discretionary writ jurisdiction of this Court, hence, this petition is dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE
